display_name: Ni superalloy substrate with bond coat and TBC

inspection_challenges: subsurface interface condition can be difficult to verify directly
thermal-cycle damage may require repeated inspection

repairability.repair_concept: Remove or locally refurbish coating layers where allowable while preserving substrate acceptance.
repair_constraints: substrate condition after stripping must be verified
bond coat and top coat repair may require qualified shop processes
likely_maintenance_actions: local blend or strip and recoat assessment

qualification_notes: Coating stack and substrate interaction require qualification as a system.
This template does not assert certified applicability for a specific component.

route_benefits: separates substrate load-bearing role from surface thermal protection
sustainability_or_cost_notes: Repair and recoat logistics can dominate through-life burden.
Process cost depends on coating route and inspection requirements.
assumptions: Substrate and coating are treated as a coupled material system.
No exact life or temperature claim is implied.
validation_gaps: coating adhesion and recession evidence for the target duty cycle

display_name: SiC/SiC CMC with environmental barrier coating

inspection_challenges: multi-layer architecture makes hidden damage difficult to characterize
EBC condition can control environmental durability

repairability.repair_concept: Repair is focused on EBC refurbishment and conservative disposition of structural CMC damage.
repair_constraints: structural CMC damage may not be fully restorable
EBC repair must preserve environmental protection

qualification_notes: Fiber, matrix, interphase and EBC must be qualified as a coupled system.
No final component certification claim is made.

sustainability_or_cost_notes: Complex processing and inspection can increase cost and lead time.
assumptions: EBC is treated as part of the material system, not an optional accessory.

inspection_challenges: porosity and fiber architecture variation may complicate acceptance criteria

repairability.repair_concept: Repair concept is conservative and likely limited to non-structural surface restoration or replacement.
repair_constraints: structural repair maturity is not assumed
fiber architecture damage may require replacement

qualification_notes: Composite variability and target environment evidence remain qualification drivers.

route_benefits: composite damage tolerance compared with monolithic ceramic baseline
